meta.title: PC Health Check — A security scanner that explains your PC
meta.description: A locale-aware diagnostic that knows Korean banking plugins. Traffic-light report, VirusTotal hash lookup, never uploads files. Free, MIT open source. Windows & macOS.

hero.title: A security scanner that explains your PC.
hero.subtitle: Generic antivirus tools flag legitimate Korean banking plugins as malware. PC Health Check recognizes IPinside, nProtect, MagicLine, V3, and surfaces only the real threats.

problem.heading: When "30 suspicious items" are flagged, which are actually threats?
problem.lead: A Korean PC is full of banking, government, and e-commerce security plugins. They all look suspicious to generic scanners, but they're legitimate. Non-technical users can't tell the difference, so they either ignore all warnings or uninstall critical software — both dangerous.

how.heading: 8 diagnostics → 1 HTML report.
how.lead: No framework. No binary. OS-specific scripts collect system state, then a cross-platform Python generator assembles a single HTML report you open in your browser.

p1.title: Collect
p1.desc: Processes, network, ports, autoruns, security posture. Sysinternals + launchd + VirusTotal hash lookup.

p2.title: Normalize
p2.desc: Match against Korean software whitelist, assign traffic-light verdict. OS-agnostic schema.

p3.title: Render
p3.desc: A single HTML file opened by the browser. No executable, no signing, no tracking.

privacy.heading: Don't take "we're safe" on faith — read the code.
privacy.lead: Every network call is in the source. VirusTotal lookups send only the SHA-256 hash; file contents never leave your machine. Here's the actual call site.

hash.title: Hash only, no file upload
hash.desc: Get-FileHash computes SHA-256 locally. The GET request carries just the hash. If the hash is unknown, we return "unknown" — we never upload the file.

telemetry.title: Zero telemetry
telemetry.desc: No usage stats, no error reports, nothing sent to us. Outbound calls are limited to opt-in VirusTotal lookups and optional Sysinternals downloads.

local.title: Local cache only
local.desc: VT responses cache in %LOCALAPPDATA% (Windows) or ~/Library/Caches (macOS) for 48h. No cloud account, no sync.

audit.title: Source you can read
audit.desc: Zero compiled binaries. ~3,700 lines of PowerShell · Python · Bash. grep -R 'Invoke-RestMethod\|Invoke-WebRequest\|urlopen' scripts/ finds every outbound call.

download.heading: Get it now.
download.lead: A single zip. Carry it on a USB drive — no installer, no admin rights needed for most checks.
download.github_side: Clone or fork: github.com/heznpc/pc-health-check. Read the ~3,700 lines yourself.

final.heading: Suspicious right now? 6 minutes to answers.
final.lead: No sign-up. No email. The report opens in your browser, and only your browser.

footer.credits: Thanks to Microsoft Sysinternals · Objective-See · the Malware Zero community.
